2605.18913.title: SCAFDS: Edge-Feature Graph Attention for Interbank Fraud Detection with Attribution-Grounded SAR Generation
2605.18913.paper_summary: The U.S. financial system processes approximately 1.3 million interbank transactions daily, yet no system in the reviewed literature models fraud propagation across the interbank network using fraud co-occurrence edge features. Prior interbank GNN architectures model credit contagion using credit distress supervision signals, producing systems misaligned for fraud forensics. No existing system generates SAR narratives with per-assertion forensic traceability to specific numerical detection outputs, creating regulatory auditability gaps in FinCEN-submitted reports. This paper introduces SCAFDS (Systemic Contagion-Aware Fraud Detection System), a seven-stage integrated surveillance pipeline addressing five structural limitations of prior art: (1) fraud-specific interbank topology encoding using fraud co-occurrence frequency metrics f(u,v,t) derived from FinCEN SAR registry records; (2) edge-feature-informed graph attention where coefficients are computed from both node representations and fraud co-occurrence edge features; (3) bilinear fraud co-occurrence risk fusion producing institution-level systemic fraud risk scores; (4) attribution-conditioned SAR narrative generation with per-assertion significance thresholds ensuring each FinCEN SAR assertion is traceable to a specific numerical pipeline output; and (5) topology-aware adaptive forensic feedback updating graph attention weights from regulatory dispositions. Experiments on the IEEE-CIS Fraud Detection Dataset (590,540 transactions) and a synthetic FDIC-aligned interbank network (8,103 institutions, 169,800 edges) show SCAFDS achieves AUPRC=0.515+/-0.032 and AUROC=0.802+/-0.018, representing +15.9pp and +13.7pp improvements over GraphSAGE-AML. Partial validation on FDIC enforcement action records (n=4,279) confirms consistent model ranking. USPTO Provisional Patent Application No. 64/061,083, filed May 8, 2026.
2605.18913.paper_summary_zh: 美國金融系統每天處理約130萬筆銀行間交易，但在已檢視的文獻中，沒有系統使用詐騙共現邊特徵來建模銀行間網絡中的詐騙傳播。先前的銀行間GNN架構使用信用困境監督信號來建模信用傳染，導致系統與詐騙取證不一致。現有系統無法生成具有每項主張法醫可追溯性的可疑活動報告（SAR）敘述，這在提交給FinCEN的報告中造成了監管審計的缺口。本文介紹了SCAFDS（系統性傳染感知詐騙檢測系統），這是一個七階段的綜合監控管道，解決了先前技術的五個結構性限制：（1）使用從FinCEN SAR登記記錄中衍生的詐騙共現頻率指標f(u,v,t)進行詐騙特定的銀行間拓撲編碼；（2）邊特徵信息的圖注意力，其中係數是從節點表示和詐騙共現邊特徵計算得出的；（3）雙線性詐騙共現風險融合，生成機構級系統性詐騙風險分數；（4）基於歸因的SAR敘述生成，具有每項主張的顯著性閾值，確保每個FinCEN SAR主張都可以追溯到特定的數值管道輸出；以及（5）拓撲感知的自適應法醫反饋，根據監管處置更新圖注意力權重。在IEEE-CIS詐騙檢測數據集（590,540筆交易）和一個合成的FDIC對齊銀行間網絡（8,103個機構，169,800條邊）上的實驗顯示，SCAFDS達到了AUPRC=0.515+/-0.032和AUROC=0.802+/-0.018，分別比GraphSAGE-AML提高了+15.9個百分點和+13.7個百分點。對FDIC執法行動記錄（n=4,279）的部分驗證確認了模型排名的一致性。美國專利商標局臨時專利申請號64/061,083，於2026年5月8日提交。
2605.18913.author: Mohammad Nasir Uddin et.al.
